description: ProgressiveTraining with single variable expands to 5 phases
input:
progressive FI_Training:
  steps v = [2, 4, 8, 12, 18]
  sessions >= 3
  stable(visual)
  FI {v}s LH3s
expected_note: ProgressiveTraining desugars to 5 phase declarations: FI_Training_1 through FI_Training_5

description: ProgressiveTraining with fixed session count (parametric dose-response)
input:
progressive DoseResponse:
  steps vol = [6, 12, 25, 50, 100, 200, 300]
  sessions = 30
  PR(exponential)
expected_note: Expands to 7 phases: DoseResponse_1 through DoseResponse_7, each with PR(exponential) and sessions=30

description: ProgressiveTraining with two variables zipped pairwise
input:
progressive AvoidanceTrain:
  steps rsi = [60, 80, 100, 120]
  steps intensity = [0.2, 0.3, 0.4, 0.5]
  sessions >= 5
  stable(visual)
  Sidman(SSI=5s, RSI={rsi}s)
expected_note: Expands to 4 phases with RSI values 60, 80, 100, 120 and intensity values 0.2, 0.3, 0.4, 0.5

description: ProgressiveTraining declaration mixed with regular phase declarations
input:
phase Pretraining:
  sessions = 5
  CRF

progressive FI_Training:
  steps v = [2, 8, 18]
  sessions >= 3
  FI {v}s

phase Terminal:
  sessions = 25
  FI18s
expected_note: Pretraining (1 phase) + FI_Training (3 expanded phases) + Terminal (1 phase) = 5 phases total

description: ProgressiveTraining with single interleave (intercalate default): 3 doses + 3 recoveries = 6 phases
input:
phase Recovery:
  sessions >= 3
  stable(visual)
  FI60s

progressive DR:
  steps v = [2, 4, 8]
  interleave Recovery
  sessions >= 5
  stable(visual)
  FI {v}s
expected_note: intercalate(Recovery, [DR_1, DR_2, DR_3]) = [DR_1, R_after_DR_1, DR_2, R_after_DR_2, DR_3, R_after_DR_3]. Recovery is template-only (does not appear standalone).

description: ProgressiveTraining with interleave + no_trailing (intersperse): 3 doses + 2 recoveries = 5 phases
input:
phase Recovery:
  sessions >= 3
  stable(visual)
  FI60s

progressive DR:
  steps v = [2, 4, 8]
  interleave Recovery no_trailing
  sessions >= 5
  stable(visual)
  FI {v}s
expected_note: intersperse(Recovery, [DR_1, DR_2, DR_3]) = [DR_1, R_after_DR_1, DR_2, R_after_DR_2, DR_3]. No trailing clone.

description: Flagship: John & Nader (2016) JEAB-style cocaine dose-response. 'each dose was followed by a return to baseline' → 6 doses + 6 recoveries = 12 phases.
input:
@species("rhesus monkey") @n(4)

phase Recovery:
  sessions >= 3
  stable(visual)
  FI600s(FR30)
  @reinforcer("cocaine", dose="0.1mg/kg")

progressive DoseResponse:
  steps dose = [0.003, 0.01, 0.03, 0.1, 0.3, 0.56]
  interleave Recovery
  sessions >= 5
  stable(visual)
  FI600s(FR30)
  @reinforcer("cocaine", dose="{dose}mg/kg")
expected_note: Method 'each dose followed by recovery' → intercalate yields 12 phases: 6 doses (with substituted dose annotation) interleaved with 6 Recovery clones (each carrying Recovery's verbatim 0.1mg/kg annotation; no progressive-side {dose} leakage).

description: Multiple interleave entries in one progressive_decl: gap block contains BOTH Recovery and Probe. 3 conditions intercalate yields 3 + 3*2 = 9 phases.
input:
phase Recovery:
  sessions >= 3
  stable(visual)
  FI60s

phase Probe:
  sessions = 1
  EXT

progressive DR:
  steps v = [2, 4, 8]
  interleave Recovery
  interleave Probe
  sessions >= 5
  stable(visual)
  FI {v}s
expected_note: Gap block = [Recovery_clone, Probe_clone] in declaration order. Result: [DR_1, R, P, DR_2, R, P, DR_3, R, P]. 9 phases.

description: Constraint 76: template phase (referenced by interleave) does not appear standalone in PhaseSequence at its declaration position. Verifies template consumption.
input:
phase Pretraining:
  sessions = 5
  CRF

phase Recovery:
  sessions >= 3
  stable(visual)
  FI60s

progressive DR:
  steps v = [2, 4]
  interleave Recovery
  sessions >= 5
  stable(visual)
  FI {v}s
expected_note: Although `phase Recovery` is declared between Pretraining and the progressive_decl, it is consumed as a template by `interleave Recovery` and does NOT appear standalone. Sequence: [Pretraining, DR_1, R_after_DR_1, DR_2, R_after_DR_2]. 5 phases.

description: Multi-variable progressive_decl (zip) composed with interleave (intercalate). Orthogonality: zip → intercalate.
input:
phase Recovery:
  sessions >= 3
  stable(visual)
  Sidman(SSI=10s, RSI=10s)

progressive AvoidanceTrain:
  steps rsi = [60, 80, 100]
  steps intensity = [0.2, 0.3, 0.4]
  interleave Recovery
  sessions >= 5
  stable(visual)
  Sidman(SSI=5s, RSI={rsi}s)
expected_note: zip yields 3 generated phases; intercalate inserts 3 Recovery clones (one between each pair AND one trailing). Total = 3 + 3 = 6 phases.

description: Property 4 (annotation locality, theory.md Definition 16): cloned Recovery phases carry the template's verbatim @reinforcer annotation; the progressive's `{dose}` placeholder does NOT leak into clones.
input:
phase Recovery:
  sessions >= 3
  stable(visual)
  FI60s
  @reinforcer("food", magnitude="45mg")

progressive DR:
  steps dose = [1, 2, 4]
  interleave Recovery
  sessions >= 5
  stable(visual)
  FI60s
  @reinforcer("drug", dose="{dose}mg/kg")
expected_note: Generated DR_i phases carry `@reinforcer(drug, dose=<value>mg/kg)`; cloned Recovery_after_DR_i phases carry `@reinforcer(food, magnitude=45mg)` verbatim. The progressive's `{dose}` placeholder is NOT substituted in clones.

description: R-4: progressive with `advance when criterion(...)` — single predicate (rate-based advancement, Eckard & Kyonka 2018 style)
input:
progressive FI_Training:
  steps v = [2, 4, 8, 12, 18]
  advance when criterion(metric="rate", threshold=>=10, window=3 sessions)
  sessions >= 5
  FI {v}s LH3s
expected_note: ProgressiveTraining expands to 5 phases; each transition gated by runtime evaluation of rate >= 10 over 3 sessions. `sessions >= 5` acts as minimum-duration floor (constraint §79: advance_condition is compatible with >=, not with exact =).

description: R-4: progressive with `advance when all(...)` — compound AND predicate (Fernando et al. 2014 avoidance acquisition: both avoidance_rate and sessions threshold required)
input:
progressive Avoidance_Acquisition:
  steps rsi = [20, 15, 10]
  advance when all(criterion(metric="avoidance_rate", threshold=>=0.8, window=3 sessions), criterion(metric="sessions", threshold=>=5, window=1 sessions))
  SidmanAv(RSI={rsi}s, SSI=5s)
expected_note: Conjunctive predicate: avoidance_rate >= 0.8 over 3 sessions AND >= 5 sessions elapsed. Flat `all(...)` — no nesting (constraint §82, design rule R2 of I-13a review).

description: R-4: progressive with `advance when manual` — experimenter-gated (Rey et al. 2019 style, where progression decisions are based on experimenter judgment)
input:
progressive Omission_Training:
  steps ratio = [5, 10, 20]
  advance when manual
  sessions = 3
  FR{ratio}
expected_note: `manual` cedes advancement to experimenter. Compatible with `sessions = N` (constraint §79 exception) because exact session count here documents observed duration, not a runtime gate.

description: R-4: sessions placeholder `{ident}` in progressive_decl (Eckard & Kyonka 2018: different sessions per FI step)
input:
progressive FI_Progressive:
  steps v = [2, 4, 8, 12, 18]
  steps s = [2, 3, 5, 10, 13]
  sessions = {s}
  FI {v}s
expected_note: 33 sessions total: FI 2s×2 + FI 4s×3 + FI 8s×5 + FI 12s×10 + FI 18s×13. Placeholder `{s}` resolves per step (constraint §81).
